title: Array Loops
description: Process all items in an array automatically.

slides.title: Looping Through an Array 🔄
slides.content:
You can loop through the array elements with the `for` loop.

Since array indices are numbers from 0 to N, a `for` loop is mathematically perfect for iterating over them!

slides.title: Example
slides.content: Using a for loop to print all cars.

quizData.question: In the example, what does cars[i] do?

options.text: It accesses the array element dynamically based on the current loop counter

quizData.explanation: As the loop increments i from 0 to 3, it asks the array for cars[0], cars[1], cars[2], and cars[3].

slides.title: The Foreach Loop 🪄
slides.content:
There is also a special "for-each" loop that is used exclusively to loop through elements in an array (or other data sets).

It is often cleaner and easier to read, because you don't need a counter variable `i`.

slides.title: For-Each Example
slides.content: This does exactly the same thing as the previous example.

quizData.question: In a for-each loop, we don't have to worry about accidentally going 'out of bounds' past the end of the array.
quizData.explanation: True! The compiler automatically iterates exactly over the items inside the array and stops safely at the end.
